fix: prefer daemon metadata over MA now-playing for track display

When playing via sourceplugin providers (e.g. Yandex ynison), MA now-playing
returns metadata from its own queue item rather than the actual playing track.
The daemon receives correct metadata via the Sendspin protocol.

Changed metadata priority in _getDeviceNowPlayingState() and
_getListTrackAlbum() to daemon-first with MA fallback, matching the existing
daemon-first logic in _getListTrackLabel() and _getListTrackArtist().

Affects: track title, artist, album, and artwork in expanded card view,
now-playing section, and all views using _getDeviceNowPlayingState().

commit.message:
fix: alarm timer package visibility (#262)

On Android 11+ (API 30+) PackageManager.resolveActivity() returns null
for implicit intents unless the target action is declared in <queries>.
Without this, setAlarm() and setTimer() in NativeIntentHandler always
hit the 'No clock app found' early-return path regardless of whether a
clock app is installed.

Fix:
- Add <queries> with SET_ALARM and SET_TIMER intent actions so
  resolveActivity() can see the system Clock app
- Add com.android.alarm.permission.SET_ALARM for OEM clock apps that
  still check this deprecated permission

Fixes #253 regression (set_timer never worked on device post-PR #257)
Fixes #259 TC-T1 to TC-T4, TC11-R, TC12-R

feat(engagement): flow detector — pure 2-state light/deep detection primitive (#141)

* feat(engagement): flow detector service (ENG-02)

Pure computational service that detects writing flow state from keystroke
timing and window focus events.

Algorithm:
- Sliding window of keystroke timestamps, bounded to retention window
- Continuous typing (gaps ≤ 30s) for 5 min → light flow
- Continuous typing for 15 min → deep flow
- Window blur → immediate flow break
- 60s silence → exit flow and reset

Key design decisions:
- Zero dependencies — pure computation, no DB/LLM/IPC
- All time values injectable via parameters (INV P4 deterministic testing)
- Keystroke buffer auto-pruned to deepThreshold + exitTimeout window
- O(n) backward scan on bounded buffer → O(1) amortized getFlowState
- Blur uses latest keystroke timestamp as chain-break marker (not
  Date.now()) to stay in the same time domain as injected timestamps

Coverage: 95% statements, 89% branches, 100% functions (18 tests)

Closes #140

* fix: address R1 audit findings for flow detector

- Change maxKeystrokeGapMs default from 30s to 15s (spec: IN_FLOW 停顿<15s)
- Fix false spec citation in flowExitTimeoutMs comment
- Fix blur boundary bug: latest keystroke now checked against lastBlurTime
- Add monotonic timestamp enforcement in recordKeystroke
- Filter future keystrokes in getFlowState (now < latest)
- Update test 14 gap from 25s to 10s, test 17 description 30s→15s
- Add 8 new edge case tests: blur before input, out-of-order timestamps,
  duplicate timestamps, future keystroke filtering, blur boundary checks

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Change > to >= for maxGap comparisons (3 sites) to match spec
  'IN_FLOW: 停顿 < 15 秒' — gap of exactly maxGap now breaks chain
- Update maxKeystrokeGapMs JSDoc to clarify >= semantics
- Clarify getFlowState now param: keystroke filtering only, not blur/focus
- Replace misleading boundary test with proper gap==maxGap + gap==maxGap-1
- Update test header comment (24 tests, 15s gap)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Add activeChainStart state to track true chain start across buffer pruning
- Fix blur sentinel from 0 to -1 to avoid timestamp-0 collision

* fix: validate exitTimeout >= maxGap to prevent chain revival

Duck R6 finding: when flowExitTimeoutMs < maxKeystrokeGapMs, getFlowState
exits flow after exitTimeout silence, but recordKeystroke does not break
the chain (gap still < maxGap). A later keystroke could then revive the
old chain and instantly report flow — semantically contradictory.

Fix: throw at construction time if exitTimeout < maxGap. Also fix the
existing exit-timeout-boundary test to satisfy the new invariant
(set maxGap = exitTimeout instead of maxGap > exitTimeout).

Added: 2 new tests (config validation: rejects invalid, accepts equal).
Tests: 33/33 green.

commit.message:
refactor(logics): remove flat schema, align deploy eos with Ansible standard model (#72)

* feat(inventory): add multi-group flat inventory schema with credential inheritance

Introduce a second inventory format (globals/groups/hosts) auto-detected
alongside the existing Ansible-style schema. Hosts can belong to multiple
groups; connection parameters (credentials, tls_verify, kind, address) are
resolved with deterministic priority: host > first-matching group > globals,
with field-by-field fallback.

## New components

- `ConnectionInventoryLoader`: schema auto-detection, flat + ansible parsing,
  credential resolution. Raises `InvalidInventoryError` on ambiguous schema
  (flat keys mixed with non-flat top-level keys).
- `ConnectionInventory` / `ResolvedHostConnection` / `ResolvedCredentials`:
  immutable value-object models for resolved connection data. Credentials
  are masked in all display paths.

## Credential key aliases supported

- `credentials.username` / `credentials.password`
- `ansible_user` / `ansible_password`
- `address` / `ansible_host`

## Commands updated

- `deploy eos`: uses ConnectionInventoryLoader; hosts without kind=arista_eos
  silently skipped; per-host tls_verify overrides global verify_ssl flag.
- `info`: displays resolved connection table (credentials masked) for flat
  schema; JSON/YAML output includes schema field.
- `validate`: detects flat schema and validates structure + resolution.

## Backward compatibility

Ansible-style inventories are unchanged. The deployer `verify_ssl` parameter
becomes Optional[bool]; None means inherit per-host setting.

commit.message:
Fix orphaned server notifications on fullSync

Two bugs caused notifications to persist on the server after being
dismissed from the phone:

1. The orphan purge in fullSync() used knownServerIds (built from the
   pre-delete local mappings) to skip entries in the server list. This
   meant any deleteNotification call that failed due to a network error
   would never be retried — the ID was in knownServerIds so the orphan
   check silently passed it. Fix: remove the knownServerIds guard and
   delete everything still on the server after clearing local mappings.

2. Race condition: activeNotifications was snapshotted at the start of
   fullSync(), but by the time postSbn() was called a notification could
   already have been dismissed. onNotificationRemoved() would fire during
   the sync but find no mapping (cleared earlier in fullSync) and return
   early — leaving the freshly-posted server entry with nothing to clean
   it up. Fix: re-snapshot activeNotifications immediately before the
   post loop and skip any keys no longer present.

Also removes the unused serverIds variable from pollServerDismissals.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: OneNote section reader — step 1 for RAG indexing (#76)

* feat: add OneNote section reader for RAG content extraction

Extract text from .one (MS-ONESTORE) binary files without requiring
OneNote to be installed or a native binary parser.

- New service: src/services/onenote-reader.ts
  - Hybrid UTF-16BE + ASCII string extraction
  - 'PageTitle' sentinel-based page boundary detection
  - Returns OneNoteSection with per-page titles, dates, and content,
    plus a combined textContent blob for whole-section RAG indexing
  - Noise filter removes font names, GUIDs, XML fragments, and
    OneNote property keys from extracted text

- New IPC handler: onedrive:read-onenote-file
  - Accepts a full file path, validates .one extension
  - Returns { ok, section } or { ok: false, error }

- Types: OneNotePageContent and OneNoteSectionContent added to
  plugins/types.ts; onedriveReadOneNoteFile added to JarvisApi

- Preload: onedriveReadOneNoteFile exposed via contextBridge

- Tests: 19 new tests using fixture .one files from the installed
  @joplin/onenote-converter package (no binary blobs committed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add OneNote folder integration tests

Proves the full group→OneDrive discovery→file scan→content extraction
pipeline using the @joplin/onenote-converter fixture files as a stand-in
OneDrive root.

- Sets up an in-memory DB group named to match a fixture subdirectory
- Runs discoverCustomerFolderForGroup, scanFilesForFolder, listFilesForFolder
- Reads every .one file found via readOneNoteSection
- Logs a formatted section/page summary (visible with --reporter=verbose)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add OneNote section viewer panel in Groups UI

When a OneDrive folder has .one files, each file now shows a 📖 button
in the expanded file list. Clicking it opens a OneNoteSectionPanel to
the right of GroupsPanel showing the extracted content.

- OneNoteSectionPanel: reads the file via onedriveReadOneNoteFile IPC,
  shows section name + page count in the header, then a card per page
  with title (heading), date, and body text (expandable past 320 chars)
- GroupsPanel: accepts onOpenOneNote callback, renders 📓 icon + 📖 button
  for .one files; constructs full path from folderPath + relativePath
- index.tsx: wires up oneNoteFilePath state, passes onOpenOneNote prop,
  renders OneNoteSectionPanel in groups-layout; clears on panel close
- onboarding.css: adds .onenote-section-panel (420px, max-height 80vh)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: read and open .url Internet Shortcut files in file list

OneNote notebooks hosted on SharePoint are represented locally as .url
shortcut files instead of .one files. This adds first-class support:

- New service src/services/url-shortcut.ts: parses the INI-format .url
  file and returns { url, isOneNote, isSharePoint }
- New IPC handler onedrive:read-url-shortcut: reads .url file -> URL
- New IPC handler shell:open-url: opens a validated https:/onenote: URL
  in the system browser/OneNote app
- GroupsPanel: pre-loads .url info when a folder expands; shows
  notebook+globe icon for OneNote links, link icon for other URLs,
  SharePoint badge, and an Open button that launches the URL

Content extraction requires Microsoft Graph API (notebook lives on
SharePoint, not locally synced). The open button is the practical
workaround until Graph API auth is added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use URL hostname parsing instead of substring check for SharePoint

CodeQL flagged incomplete URL substring sanitization: using .includes()
allows spoofing via paths or subdomains (e.g. evil.com/sharepoint.com).

Replace with proper URL parsing: check parsed.hostname equals or ends
with '.sharepoint.com' to safely identify SharePoint-hosted links.

* feat(06-03): fix dark mode inputs and hydration warnings — pre-checkpoint audit

All 5 acceptance criteria verified at 76b46b3:
- layout.tsx: suppressHydrationWarning on both html AND body (2 matches)
- LoginForm.tsx: dark:bg-gray-800/dark:border-gray-600 on both inputs
- RegisterForm.tsx: dark:bg-gray-800/dark:border-gray-600 on all 3 inputs
- TopNav.tsx: all nav strings via useT() — 14 matches
- login/page.tsx: dark:text-gray-400 on paragraph text

Fixes were already incorporated via imp/i18n + fix/polish merges in phase 06-01.

* fix(06-03): eager-load i18n dicts + add footer links to privacy/terms

- Replace async lazy-load in useT() with eager imports of fr/de dicts.
  The lazy approach called setLocale(sameLocale) after loading which
  Zustand ignored (no state change), so FR/DE stayed in English.
- Add footer to root layout with Privacy Policy and Terms of Service links
  (previously only accessible from login/home pages)

* fix(06-03): redirect authenticated users to /dashboard from privacy/terms back link

* gitignore next-env.d.ts

* fix(06-03): propagate LLM provider errors instead of swallowing them

- Add ProviderError exception class to llm_service with provider/status/detail
- call_custom_provider now raises ProviderError on non-200 or unexpected exception
  instead of silently returning None — logs the status+body for debugging
- Both summary and hint routes catch ProviderError and raise HTTP 502 with detail
- Frontend shows resp.data.detail on error instead of generic 'unavailable' string

* fix(llm): skip response validation for user-supplied API keys

validate_response rejects responses >500 chars or containing '<'.
For custom provider calls the user pays their own costs so the
platform's budget-protection limits don't apply.

* fix(llm): remove 500-char response length limit from validate_response

* untrack next-env.d.ts

* fix(06-03): fix ThemeToggle hydration mismatch — defer icon until mounted

* fix(06-03): replace next-themes with custom theme store to fix React 19 script warning

next-themes 0.4.6 injects a <script> inside a client component which
React 19 warns about. Replaced with:
- src/store/theme.ts: Zustand store with localStorage persistence + DOM class sync
- layout.tsx: inline blocking script in <head> (server component, no warning)
- ThemeProvider.tsx: syncs store to DOM class via useEffect
- TopNav ThemeToggle: uses useThemeStore instead of useTheme

* fix(06-03): defer locale in useT() until mounted to fix SSR hydration mismatch

Server always renders in 'en'; if user has 'de'/'fr' in localStorage
the client would mismatch on hydration. Use mounted flag so the first
render matches the server, then switch to the stored locale after mount.

commit.message:
fix: unhandled actions in GraphicsPath.transform() switch statement (#11996)

* Initial plan

* fix: handle closePath, regularPoly, and chamferRect in GraphicsPath.transform

Add missing cases to the transform() switch statement:
- closePath: no-op (no data to transform)
- regularPoly: adjust transform at data[5]
- chamferRect: adjust transform at data[5]

This prevents the "unknown transform action closePath" warning and properly
handles transforms for regularPoly and chamferRect shapes.

Fixes #11184

- ScanStore passed as @EnvironmentObject for reactive view updates
